data.title: U.S. Futures Edge Up, European Stocks Mostly Down
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were up 0.1% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.1%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 declined 0.1% in morning trading. Delivery Hero added 5.5% and Mowi rose 2.3%. On the other hand, Whitbread slipped 7.2%, and Puma fell 3.1%. The FTSE 100 added 0.1%. Other stocks in Europe were down as France's CAC 40 decreased 0.1% and Germany's DAX dropped 0.2%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index rose 0.1% to 97.07.

In commodities, Brent crude gained 0.3% to $63.09 a barrel, and WTI crude climbed 0.2% to $59.20 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 1% to 29.47 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield climbed by 1 basis point to 2.69% from 2.685%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury rose by 1 basis point to 4.012% from 3.999%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were mixed as Japan's Nikkei 225 index gained 0.2%, whereas Hong Kong's Hang Seng fell 0.3%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite gained 0.3%.


-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.


Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-28-25 0349ET

data.title: Meituan books 2% rise in third-quarter revenue but net income slumps
data.description:
BEIJING (Reuters) -China's leading food delivery group, Meituan, reported on Friday a 2% rise in third-quarter revenue, beating analyst estimates, as it intensifies efforts to compete for market share in the instant retail sector, where goods are delivered within an hour.

(Reporting by Beijing Newsroom; Editing by Jacqueline Wong)

Check Point expands sports cybersecurity
data.description:
Check Point, founded in 1993 and headquartered in Tel Aviv, Israel, provides cybersecurity solutions including network, cloud, and endpoint security software, cloud-native protection, and security management platforms for enterprises across Americas, primarily the U.S., Europe, Middle East and Africa, Israel, and Asia Pacific.

Elevating sports cybersecurity

On October 27, 2025, Check Point announced the long-term partnership with Harris Blitzer Sports & Entertainment (HBSE) and Washington Commanders to ensure cyber protection for premier sports properties like the Philadelphia 76ers (NBA), the New Jersey Devils (NHL), Prudential Center in Newark, etc. This collaboration would enable Check Point to strengthen its cybersecurity defense across HBSE digital platforms, ensuring brand visibility and fan engagement, and expand worldwide through HSBE's diverse portfolio and customized offerings.

Continued growth sustenance

Check Point posted decent performance over FY 21-24, achieving a revenue CAGR of 5.8%, reaching 2.6 billion US dollars in FY 24, propelled by rising adoption for cybersecurity solutions, expansion in cloud security, and strategic investments. EBIT registered a CAGR of -1.2%, reaching USD 876.0m. Consequently, margins contracted from 41.9% to 34.2%.

Over FY 21-24, cash and cash equivalent grew from USD 272.0m to USD 506.0m. In addition, ROE improved from 24.3% to 30.2%.

The company presented strong Q3 25 results, driven by robust billings growth supported by strong demand for External Risk Management (ERM), Hybrid-Mesh-Network, and Workspace portfolios. In addition, Check Point acquired its first AI-native security platform- "Lakera", boosting its position to provide top-notch AI-powered security platforms. However, operating margin contracted by 502bp to 29.4%.

In comparison, Fortinet, Inc., a local peer, reported a revenue CAGR of 21.2% over FY 21-24, reaching USD 6.0bn in FY 24. EBIT grew at CAGR of 40.7% to USD 1.8bn, with margin expansion from 19.3% to 30.2%.

Positive outlook

Over the past year, the company's stock delivered returns of 1.5%. In comparison, Fortinet posted negative returns of 14.4%.

Check Point is currently trading at a P/E of 20.6x, based on the FY 25 estimated EPS of USD 9.0, which is higher than its 3-year historical average of 22.2x but lower than Fortinet’s valuation of 33.7x. The company is currently trading at an EV/EBIT multiple of 15.1x, based on FY 25 estimated EBIT of USD 1.1bn, which is higher than its 3-year historical average of 14.0x but lower than Fortinet (24.3x).

Check Point is monitored by 37 analysts, 15 of whom have ‘Buy’ ratings and 22 have ‘Hold’ ratings for an average target price of USD 228.4, implying an upside of 23.9% over the current market price.

The analysts’ views are supported by an estimated EBIT CAGR of 4.1% over FY 24-27, reaching USD 1.2bn, with margin of 40.5% in FY 27. In addition, analysts estimate a net profit CAGR of 2.9% to USD 922.5m. Likewise, for Fortinet, the analysts estimate an EBIT CAGR of 11.2% and net profit CAGR of 8.3% over FY 24-27.

Overall, Check Point presented steady operational resilience and strategic advancement through robust demand for its cybersecurity offerings, successful acquisitions, and partnerships that expand its vertical reach. The company’s outlook remains compelling, supported by a sustainable income growth forecast. However, it may face risks from rapid technological changes, heightened competition, geopolitical uncertainty, and evolving cyber threats.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks End Week Lower as Winning Run Stalls; Haiwei Electronic New Material Slips on Debut
Tech titan's triumph: Apple's growth and global grasp
data.description:
Apple, which was founded in 1976 and is headquartered in Cupertino, California, designs, manufactures, and markets computer hardware and music supports. Net sales include iPhone (51.4%), peripherals (9.5%), computers (7.7%), music support (6.8%), and other services (24.6%). Sales distribution: Americas (42.7%), China/Hong Kong/Taiwan (17.1%), Japan (6.4%), Asia/Pacific (7.9%), Europe/India/Middle East/Africa (25.9%).

Apple’s achievement of capturing one-fourth of China’s smartphone market in October 2025 is a testament to its enduring appeal and strong brand loyalty in a highly competitive landscape. With a market share of 23.82%, Apple has outpaced rivals like Huawei, Xiaomi, and Vivo, reflecting robust consumer demand for its latest iPhone models and ecosystem integration.

This milestone highlights Apple’s ability to maintain premium positioning and drive innovation, even amid economic headwinds and local competition. The result underscores Apple’s global strength and its strategic success in China, one of the world’s most important technology markets, positioning the company for continued growth and influence in the region.

Gearing improved

Apple Inc. has posted a revenue CAGR of 1.8% over FY 22-25, reaching 416.0 billion US Dollar, driven by Services segment outperformance and growth in iPhone and Mac sales. EBIT rose at a CAGR of 3.7%, reaching USD 133bn, with margins expanding by 168bp to 32.0%.

Over FY 22-25, cash and cash equivalent rose from USD 23.7bn to USD 35.9bn. Total debt declined from USD 132bn to USD 112bn. Consequently, its gearing was reduced from 261.5% to 152.4%.

In comparison, Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd., a global peer, reported a lower revenue CAGR of 2.5% to USD 203.7bn over FY 21-24. However, EBIT dropped at a CAGR of minus 14.1% to USD 22.2bn, with margins contracting from 18.5% to 10.9%.

Compelling prospects

Over the past year, the company's stock has delivered returns of approximately 18.1%, lower than that of Samsung Electronics, which delivered returns of 83.8%. The company paid an annual dividend of USD 1.0 in FY 25, resulting in a dividend yield of 0.4%.

Apple Inc. is currently trading at a P/E of 33.6x, based on the FY 26 estimated EPS of USD 8.3, which is higher than its 3-year historical average of 33.2x and that of Samsung Electronics (P/E of 18.4x). Regarding EV/EBIT, the company is currently trading at 28.1x, based on FY 26 estimated EBIT of USD 144.6bn, which is higher than its 3-year historical average of 26.4x and that of Samsung Electronics (15.0x).

Apple Inc. is monitored by 44 analysts, with 29 having 'Buy' ratings and 15 having 'Hold' ratings, with an average target price of USD 281.8, implying 1.5% upside potential from its current price.

These views are further supported by an anticipated EBIT CAGR of 8.5% to USD 169.9bn over FY 25-28, with margins expanding by 130bp to 33.3%. Net income is estimated to rise at a CAGR of 8.4% to USD 142.6bn. Likewise, for Samsung Electronics, analysts estimate an EBIT CAGR of 39.7% and a net profit CAGR of 27.9% over FY 24-27.

Overall, Apple strong market position, innovation, and financial performance, particularly in China, highlight its resilience and growth potential. Apple's strategic success and robust analyst support position it well for continued influence and expansion in the global technology market. However, Apple faces regulatory, operational, competitive, geopolitical, reputational, macroeconomic, and environmental risks, including scrutiny, supply chain issues, market dependence, innovation challenges, and sustainability expectations.

Enel relaunches bid for electricity concessions in Brazil
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Enel aims to strengthen its presence in the electricity distribution business in Brazil, a country to which it has allocated investments of EUR5 billion for the period 2025-2027.

As reported on Friday by MF-Milano Finanza, after receiving approval in August from Aneel for a 30-year extension of Enel's concession in Rio de Janeiro, the group led by Flavio Cattaneo is now looking at early renewal in the São Paulo area as well. Enel Distribuição São Paulo's concession serves over 8.2 million users in 24 municipalities in the Metropolitan Region, including the capital, over more than 4,500 km of network, and expires on June 15, 2028. Enel has already submitted an application for early renewal under the regulations governing 19 concessions expiring between 2025 and 2031.

The situation is different in Rio de Janeiro, where the concession, which expires in December 2026, covers approximately 73% of the state and serves 3.1 million customers. Enel Distribuição Ceará's contract, which serves over 4 million customers, is also awaiting ANEEL's opinion and expires in 2026. In the case of São Paulo, in September, ANEEL's technical departments issued a preliminary favorable opinion, considering that the criteria on economic and financial balance, service quality, investments, and operational commitments had been met.

However, the process was blocked after the intervention of the Federal Public Ministry and a subsequent decision that required ANEEL to temporarily suspend the procedure, requesting the introduction of new indicators to prevent blackouts and service disruptions. As indicated in Enel Américas' financial report, the renewal of Enel São Paulo is currently suspended as a result of a decision by the Federal Supreme Court and is the subject of an appeal.

In the meantime, Enel São Paulo has relaunched with the "Plano Verão 2025" (Summer Plan 2025), presented in November, which provides for a strengthening of operational teams and an acceleration of interventions on the grid to cope with extreme weather events, as part of a record plan of approximately EUR1.9 billion, equal to approximately BRL10.4 billion. The company claims to have complied with all the requirements of the decree for early renewal. In Rio, too, the strategy has led to an increase in investments to EUR964 million over the three-year period 2025-2027, up 74% on the previous plan.

The Minister of Mines and Energy, Alexandre Silveira, also weighed in on the issue, asking that the renewal not be politicized and reiterating his preference for extending existing concessions, because a new tender could lead to "delays and potential negative impacts on service continuity." Rio's precedent remains relevant, after ANEEL has already recommended a 30-year extension, now under final review by the ministry.

By Antonio Di Giorgio, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

CIMB Group Holdings Berhad Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
CIMB Group Holdings Berhad reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported net income was MYR 2,077.96 million compared to MYR 2,030.36 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.1932 compared to MYR 0.1898 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.1928 compared to MYR 0.1888 a year ago.
For the nine months, net income was MYR 5,940.08 million compared to MYR 5,927.67 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.5528 compared to MYR 0.5549 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.5513 compared to MYR 0.5524 a year ago.

data.title: Hong Kong authorities: Firefighting operation almost complete
data.description:
STORY: :: Hong Kong

:: November 28, 2025

:: With firefighting operations nearly complete, attention now turns to search and rescue at Wang Fuk Court

:: Derek Armstrong Chan, Deputy Director of Fire Services

"Our firefighting operation is almost complete. Despite that, we are still carrying out firefighting operation to four individual units and damping down to various units of the seven groups are still in progress. So as to prevent the debris and embers from flaring up. What's next is the search and rescue operation. We will conduct such an operation to the the remaining 25 assisting course. And at the same time, we were endeavoured to effect forcible entry to all the units of the seven blocks concerned so as to ensure that there are no other possible casualties."

According to an official, the blaze killed at least 79 people with scores more listed as missing.

Authorities later updated the death toll to 83 following the news conference in this edit. 

It is Hong Kong's deadliest fire since 1948, when 176 people were killed in a warehouse blaze.

The government said in the very early hours of Thursday that 279 people were listed as missing, but that figure has not been updated for 24 hours.

Citigroup Selected by Saudi Aramco for Oil Storage Terminals Stake Sale Help
Hong Kong nears end of Wang Fuk Court search and rescue mission
data.description:
STORY: EDITORS PLEASE NOTE: THIS STORY HAS BEEN REFILED TO REMOVE AN OUTDATED LINE IN THE STORY'S TEXT ABOUT THE SEARCH AND RESCUE OPERATIONS EXPECTED TO BE COMPLETED BY 9AM.

:: Hong Kong

:: November 28, 2025

:: Wang Fuk Court residents hope for more survivors as the search and rescue mission nears its end

"Hope they can find more survivors in the building, I think they had tried their best, the firefighters had done a lot. It is a terrible disaster that no one wanted to happen."

"Very sad because I have lived here for over 30 years."

REPORTER ASKING: "DO YOU KNOW ANYONE WHO LIVES HERE? OR ANY RELATIVES?"

"Yes. My colleague and we couldn't contact him until now."

Soon after dawn on Friday, firefighters had mostly contained the blaze that destroyed the Wang Fuk Court housing complex in the northern district of Tai Po. The eight-tower estate housing more than 4,600 people had been undergoing renovations and was wrapped in bamboo scaffolding and green mesh.

As many as 279 people were listed as missing in the early hours of Thursday (November 27) morning, but that figure has not been updated for more than 24 hours. A fire official said 25 calls for help to the Fire Department remain unresolved, including three in recent hours which would be prioritized.

data.title: Indonesia palm oil shipments deferred to December in expectation of export tax cut
CME Data-Center Issue Hits Options, Futures Trading -- Update
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao


Trading of futures and options on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange was halted for hours on Friday due to a data-center problem.

"Due to a cooling issue at CyrusOne data centers, our markets are currently halted," according to CME Group's website.

"Support is working to resolve the issue in the near term and will advise clients of pre-open details as soon as they are available," it said.

CME Group is one of the world's largest derivatives marketplace, operating exchanges including the New York Mercantile Exchange, Chicago Board of Trade, and the Commodity Exchange, also known as Comex.

People familiar with the situation said that Comex and Nymex markets have all been affected.

That has halted trading in contracts such as U.S. crude oil, palm oil and soybean oil.

U.S. markets were closed on Thursday for the Thanksgiving holiday.

"Liquidity is already thin, so even a brief halt can distort price discovery in Treasuries, foreign exchange and commodities," said Charu Chanana, chief investment strategist at Saxo Singapore.

Trading on Bursa Malaysia's derivatives market have also been halted as its products trade on CME Group's electronic trading platform, the Malaysian exchange said in a statement.

"We are actively managing the situation and are working with CME Group to restore services as quickly as possible. Our priority is to minimize impact and ensure market integrity," it added.

Front-month WTI and Brent crude-oil futures were last trading around $59 and $64 a barrel respectively before the halt, according to the ICE platform.

"The timing is particularly unnerving for traders - we're at month-end, and a few contracts are facing their final trading day today," said Priyanka Sachdeva, senior market analyst at Phillip Nova.

"Once trading resumes, we could see a sharp rush to unwind or roll positions, which may inject a burst of volatility and even some knee-jerk, irrational price moves as participants scramble to normalize risk," Sachdeva added.


Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-28-25 0315ET

StanChart Buys Back Shares
Indonesian town buries its dead in mass grave as flood toll rises
data.description:
BATANG TORU, Indonesia (Reuters) -Families gathered at a health centre in the Indonesian town of Batang Toru on Friday, desperate for news about missing loved ones, as locals dug a mass grave to bury seven unclaimed victims of the cyclonic rains that have battered the country's western-most island of Sumatra.

The rains caused floods and landslides in three provinces across Sumatra island, killing at least 94 people, according to Indonesia's disaster agencies. Nearly 80,000 people have been evacuated and about 80 people are still missing.

In Batang Toru, where at least 21 have died, residents lifted seven decomposing bodies, wrapped in black plastic, from the back of a truck onto a wide plot of land belonging to a state plantation firm, while an excavator dumped clumps of dirt to bury them in a mass grave.

"We found 21 bodies that were placed in a health centre ... the seven will be buried," said Mara Tinggi, chief of Batang Toru town, which is located in the worst-hit province of North Sumatra.

There is no morgue to store the bodies to wait for loved ones to arrive, residents told Reuters. Many at the health centre, surrounded by felled trees, had to hold their noses because of the stench of the decomposing bodies.

Indonesian authorities are rushing to respond to the worst rains and floods to hit the region in years. The death toll has risen sharply over the last 24 hours, and officials fear it may increase further.

Road access remains minimal and critical infrastructure, including power and communications, has been severely affected, the disaster mitigation agency said.

The agency said the search for the missing continued on Friday. Authorities were working to restore power and clear roads that have been blocked by landslide debris.

In West Sumatra, over 100 people still remain stranded in their homes, awaiting help and aid, said Ilham Wahab, spokesperson for the local search and rescue agency.

Floods across Southeast Asia, which also hit Thailand and Malaysia, have per Friday killed 183 people.

(Reporting by Yudhistira in Batang Toru; Writing by Stanley Widianto; Editing by Gibran Peshimam)

Alibaba enters the smart glasses market with Qwen AI
data.description:
On Thursday Alibaba launched its first artificial intelligence-powered smart glasses, called Quark AI Glasses, marking a new step in its strategy to expand into consumer AI. Available in two models, the S1 at 3,799 yuan (around $536) and the G1 at 1,899 yuan (around $270), the glasses aim to compete with the Ray-Ban Display from Meta and EssilorLuxottica, which have been on the market since September. Equipped with the Qwen models developed by Alibaba, they enable voice interactions with the AI, instant translation and the generation of summaries.

The Quark AI Glasses feature displays embedded in the lenses and an onboard camera, with a notable function that can recognize a product and display its price via Taobao. The S1 model stands out for its superior display quality. Initially intended for the Chinese market, these glasses are already facing local competitors such as Xiaomi and Xreal. According to Omdia, global sales of smart glasses could exceed 10 million units in 2026.

This launch is part of a broader push by Alibaba to invest in artificial intelligence. Its Qwen app has surpassed 10 million downloads in one week. Meanwhile, the group's cloud division, the engine of its AI growth, has recently seen a sharp increase in revenue. Determined to establish itself against Tencent and Baidu, Alibaba intends to capitalize on this new generation of devices to accelerate the rollout of its AI solutions to the mass market.
